threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You'll know they're new when they say they love the 'quirky' coffee shop but haven't tried the one on the corner that closes at 2 PM.
Heard two lads on the 133 discussing the best chicken spots in Clapham. One swore by the late-night delivery from that old café on the high street.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard the unmistakable sound of a street vendor shouting ‘Bier! Currywurst!’ while dodging a guy on a bike in the middle of a protest.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find late-night kebab shops blasting techno, where the staff know your order before you even step inside. It's a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to avoid BART at rush hour when the Transbay Tube's a mess. Out-of-towners just wonder why it always feels like a Friday evening commute.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Noticed a guy with a full drum kit on the 19 bus yesterday, just chilling and jamming while we all tried to pretend it was normal.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just about Gaudí; they miss our hidden plazas where locals sip vermut, swapping stories well into the night.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their hesitation at the crosswalks—waiting too long because they’re not used to the pedestrian lights here.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions to the Tube instead of just saying you're catching the Overground from the Dalston Junction.
Caught a peek at a guy in a floral shirt serenading a bus full of half-asleep commuters at 2 AM. Only in Peckham, right? Made my night.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw an old lady haggling over a watermelon on Sonnenallee, while a punk juggled in the background. Classic Neukölln!

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a club event that starts at noon and still goes strong until Monday morning, with brunch specials served at 4 AM.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they sidestep the BART delays—while newcomers look lost, the regulars know the shortcut to the bus.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 6 today, calmly reading Shakespeare. Just another Muni Tuesday, I guess.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the locals hang out at the big squares, but you'll find us tucked away in the little bars, especially on a Tuesday.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by the way they still get off at Diagonal instead of taking the shortcut through the park to Gràcia. Classic rookie move.
